steps.n: Added نا to show 'we' are doing the action. This word is part of a pair with صَبَرْنَا (we were patient), asking which response they had.

steps.m: or (connecting two equal alternatives in a question)
steps.n: This is a question particle that connects two equal choices. Here it connects أَجَزِعْنَآ (whether we panic) with صَبَرْنَا (or we are patient) in the ayah.
Basic meaning relates to patience
Basic past tense verb showing someone being patient

steps.m: we endured patiently (contrasted with panicking in أَجَزِعْنَآ)
steps.n: The نَا shows this is 'we' and makes this verb part of a comparison between two responses: panicking (أَجَزِعْنَآ) or being patient (صَبَرْنَا)
Negative particle used here to negate the possibility of escape mentioned in the following phrase. In this context, it means 'there is no escape for us' rather than being a question word.
This is a basic preposition particle that shows direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next part
Added نَا which means 'us' to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to مَحِيصٍ (escape/refuge) in the next ayah to mean 'escape for us'

steps.m: from (away from عَذَابِ ٱللَّهِ - the punishment of Allah)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing separation or distance. In this ayah, it shows the weak followers asking if the arrogant leaders can protect them from Allah's punishment.
This is the basic meaning showing movement away from something

steps.m: a place to move away to
steps.n: Adding مَ at the start turns this into a noun meaning the place where the action happens

steps.m: definitely a place to move away to
steps.n: Adding shadda on م makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'

steps.m: any place to move away to (after the word مِن)
steps.n: Adding ٍ at the end shows this word comes after مِن (from) and means 'any' instead of 'the'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter. This word starts what Satan is saying about Allah.

steps.m: and he said (subject of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added ي after first letter to make it mean 'one who is rebellious/distant' (like turning 'rebel' into 'rebel-er')
Added ان to make it mean someone who is very rebellious (like adding 'super-' to 'rebel')
Added ال to make it specific (like adding 'the' in English)
Adding -u ending to show this word is doing the action (like adding 'the' in English)
Starting with the basic components: لَ (indeed/surely) and مَا (what/that)
Adding shadda to combine the particles creates a new meaning indicating 'when' something happens. Here it introduces what happens when the matter is decided (قُضِىَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it. This word is what was 'said' in the phrase 'and Satan said when it was decided.'
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is in the nominative case, like the subject of a sentence
Adding ال makes it definite, and the ُ shows it's the subject
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to show this is the basic form of the emphasis particle.
Added shadda (ّ) to make the word stronger. This shows strong emphasis, making the statement that follows more certain and important. In this ayah, it emphasizes Allah's promise.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that refers specifically to the One True God.
Added shadda to emphasize the ل and fatha at the end because it's the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Changed alif to wasl form since it comes after a word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb meaning 'he promised'

steps.m: he promised you all (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show that the promise was made to multiple people. This word is part of what الشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan) said, as introduced by قَالَ (he said) earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'promise'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed) in the phrase 'indeed Allah promised you'
Connected the letters together and added basic vowel marks to make it pronounceable
The second ق is combined with the first one using shadda (ّ) to show emphasis
Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the truth' instead of just 'truth'

steps.m: of the truth (possessed by وَعْدَ - promise)
steps.n: Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to وَعْدَ (promise), making the phrase 'promise of truth'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action
Added shadda on ت for pronunciation when connecting to next suffix
Added كُمْ to show who was promised (you all)
Added وَ at beginning to connect with previous statement (إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَعَدَكُمْ - Indeed Allah promised you)

steps.m: to break a promise, to fail to fulfill

steps.n: Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to cause something to break/fail' - like actively breaking a promise

steps.m: I broke/failed to fulfill (the promise to وَعَدتُّكُمْ)
steps.n: Adding ت shows that Satan (I) did this action. This relates back to Satan's earlier promise mentioned in وَعَدتُّكُمْ (I promised you)

steps.m: I broke my promise to you all
steps.n: كم shows this was done to multiple people - referring to all those who followed Satan

steps.m: So I broke my promise to you all
steps.n: فَ connects this to the previous statement about Allah's true promise (وَعْدَ ٱلْحَقِّ) versus Satan's false promise
This is a negative particle that makes sentences negative. In this ayah, it negates 'I had any authority over you'
The وَ prefix connects this phrase to what came before it, showing this is an additional statement by الشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan)
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
Added kasra to the letter lam to connect it with what follows
Added ي at the end to show this means 'me'

steps.m: to/for me (object of فَٱسْتَجَبْتُمْ - you responded)
steps.n: Changed the writing style of ي to ى with fatha for clarity in the Quran. This word shows who was responded to in فَٱسْتَجَبْتُمْ (you responded).
This is the basic form showing the concept of being above or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: upon you all (being addressed by الشَّيْطٰنُ)
steps.n: The كُم suffix shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to. In this ayah, Satan (الشَّيْطٰنُ) is addressing a group of people.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. It's like saying 'from' or 'of' in English.
Added a strong emphasis (shadda) on the م because it comes after a pause in speech. In this ayah, it connects to the word سُلْطٰنٍ (authority), saying 'any authority over you.'
Added damma and sukun to create basic noun pattern
Added ان at the end to show completeness of authority
Added the two dots below (tanween) to show it means 'any authority' since it comes after مِن
This is a basic particle used to exclude something from what came before it. In this ayah, it excludes 'calling you' from Shaytan's power over people.
The shadda emphasizes the exclusion, making it stronger and more definite
The alif is lengthened at the end because this word comes at the end of a phrase, making it easier to pause here when reading
This particle connects دَعَوْتُكُمْ (I called you) to what follows. It shows what the calling/invitation was about. It's like saying 'I called you to [do something]' in English.
The و at the end changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb
The ا changes back to و before adding endings. The sukoon (ْ) prepares it for the next part.
Added تُ to show 'I' did the action in the past
Added كُمْ to show the action was done to 'you all' (when speaking to a group)
Added است at the beginning to show answering/responding to someone's specific request or call
Added تُمْ to show multiple people being addressed

steps.m: then you all responded (to دَعَوْتُكُمْ - I called you)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of the previous action (being called). This word relates to دَعَوْتُكُمْ (I called you) in the ayah.
Basic preposition that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: to/for me (connects to استجبتم - you responded)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this affects 'me'. In this ayah, Shaytan is saying he had no authority over them except that he called them and they responded to him.

steps.m: do not (command to stop blaming me)
steps.n: This is the basic negative command word that tells someone to stop doing something. Here it's telling them to stop blaming.

steps.m: so do not (command to stop blaming me)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this command comes as a result of what was said before. The Shaytan is saying 'since I had no real power over you except calling you, so don't blame me'
Changed root letters into a verb by adding the vowel marks 'u' (damma)
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the blaming
Added ى at the end to show that 'me' is being blamed
Changed from basic root to a command by adding vowel marks - telling one person to blame
Added وا to make the command for multiple people
Added و with fatha to connect this command with what came before it

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, have spirit

steps.n: Adds vowels to make a noun meaning the essence or spirit of something. In this ayah, it's what should be blamed according to the command لُومُوا (blame).
Changes نَفْس (one self) to its plural form by adding أ at the start and changing the middle vowels
The word 'selves' gets كُم ('your') at the end to mean 'yourselves', and the vowel marks change to show it's being acted upon
This is the basic negative particle that makes something negative, like adding 'not' in English
Adding shadda on م makes the negation stronger and more emphatic
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) instead of regular ا. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic form of the personal pronoun meaning 'I'
Added alif at the end to strengthen the pronoun
Added a special mark (۠) to show emphasis and contrast with others in the context. In this ayah, Satan is emphasizing himself in contrast to others.

steps.m: to cry out for help or rescue someone
steps.n: This root relates to calling out loudly, especially for help or assistance

steps.m: one who helps/assists/responds to a cry for help
steps.n: Adds مُ at the start to show who does the action (the helper) and adds ِ after ر to complete the pattern. This changes it from the action of helping to the person who helps.
The بِ (bi) at the start means 'with/by/as' - it shows this helper is being used as a tool or means

steps.m: as your (all of you) helper (in response to وَمَآ أَنتُم)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show this refers to all the people being addressed. This word appears in response to وَمَآ أَنتُم (and you are not), showing who cannot help.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'. The alif is written long (آ) to show it's at the end of a word.

steps.m: and not (connected to بِمُصْرِخِكُمْ - your helper)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. In this ayah, Shaytan is saying 'and I am not your helper'.
This is the base form of the independent pronoun for addressing someone
Adding م at the end makes it refer to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: to scream or cry out for help
steps.n: This root relates to calling out loudly, especially for help or assistance
Changes the root 'to scream for help' into 'one who helps' by adding mu- at the start, showing who does the action
Adds the preposition ب meaning 'with' or 'by' to indicate instrumentality or accompaniment
The ي suffix makes it possessive - 'my helper'

steps.m: with my helper/rescuer (in response to أَنَا۠)
steps.n: Added shadda and fatha on final ي for emphasis. This word is part of the phrase 'I am not your helper' (مَّآ أَنَا۠ بِمُصْرِخِكُمْ) in the ayah.
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows

steps.m: indeed I (speaking in the ayah: الشَّيْطٰنُ - Satan)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show this is about 'me' (the speaker). In this ayah, Satan is speaking about himself.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the ending to show that 'I' did this action. In this ayah, this is Satan speaking about himself.
Added fatha to show the correct pronunciation
Added the preposition بِ (with) at the beginning to show how something is done
Changed the spelling of ا to آ, which is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Noun form meaning the act of associating partners with God
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at start makes it causative - doing the action to someone else
Added تُ to show first person past tense
Added و to show 'him' as the object

steps.m: you all made me a partner
steps.n: Added نِ at the end to show 'me' as the object (the one being made a partner with)
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with قَبْلُ (before) to show something happened 'from before'.
This form makes it mean a time or place before something
Adding these vowel marks turns it into a noun meaning 'before'
Adding the damma (U sound) at the end shows this word is in the nominative case

This is an emphatic particle that makes what follows more certain and important. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah truly did make a promise. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show this emphasis.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he did) to a noun meaning the person who does the action. Like changing 'he wronged' to 'wrongdoer'
Changed from singular (one person) to plural (many people) by adding ين at the end. The ين ending shows it's talking about multiple people.

steps.m: the people who act unfairly (acted on by إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ظ letter. This word is affected by إِنَّ which came before it in the verse.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: for them (who will have عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (painful punishment) in the next ayah, showing who will receive the punishment.
Basic meaning of causing pain or punishment
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning the actual punishment itself. This word works with أَلِيمٌ (painful) to describe the type of punishment.
Adding the double dammah makes this an indefinite noun - meaning 'a punishment' rather than 'the punishment'
The ي between second and third root letters makes it an intensive adjective, here describing the punishment (عَذَابٌ)
Adding tanween (double dammah) makes the word indefinite, meaning 'a painful' rather than 'the painful'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show someone was made to enter rather than entering by themselves

steps.m: and was made to enter (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this action with the previous one (believed)
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال at beginning with shadda on ل to make it specific
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word describes those who believed (ءَامَنُوا۟) in this ayah.
Adding alif makes it mean 'to believe' instead of just 'to feel secure'

steps.m: they believed (connected to وَعَمِلُوا۟ - and they did)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people did this action together. This word connects to وَعَمِلُوا۟ (and they did) in the ayah, showing these are the same group of people doing both actions.

steps.m: to do good deeds, to work righteously

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form, meaning he did it
Adding وا changes the subject from one male to a group of males (they)

steps.m: and they did good deeds (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ - they believed)
steps.n: Adding و connects this action with the previous verb ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed), showing these people both believed AND did good deeds
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form describing someone/something that has the quality of being good
Added feminine plural ending to refer to multiple good deeds or actions. This word is what was done (object) in عَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَات (they did good deeds).
Added the definite article ال. The ل assimilates into ص with a shadda because ص is a sun letter.
The alif in 'good deeds' is written as a long mark (ٰ) and gets a kasra ending because it's receiving the action 'they did'

steps.m: to cover or hide (like a garden being hidden by trees)

steps.n: Adding vowels makes it a proper past tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to cover' into a noun meaning 'garden' by doubling the middle letter and adding ة at the end
Changed singular garden to plural gardens using the feminine plural ending ات

steps.m: gardens (being entered into by الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of أُدْخِلَ (was admitted into)
Basic past tense verb meaning 'flowed' - like water moving
Added تَ at start to show feminine subject, changed vowels to show present tense. This word describes الْأَنْهٰرُ (the rivers) which is feminine plural in Arabic.

This is a particle that shows where something starts from or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the verb تَجْرِى (flows) with تَحْتِهَا (beneath it) to show that the rivers flow from beneath the gardens.
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'below'
Added ها which refers back to the gardens (جَنَّات) mentioned earlier. The ِ (kasra) is added before ها to connect it smoothly.
Basic noun form showing the place or result of flowing
Broken plural form showing multiple rivers
Adding ال makes it specific rivers rather than just any rivers
The ُ shows these rivers are doing the flowing (تَجْرِى) in this verse
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'he remained' to noun meaning 'one who remains' by adding alif after first letter

steps.m: those who will remain forever (describes الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ين to show this describes multiple people. The word describes 'those who believed' (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟) when they enter Paradise.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show the basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added ها at the end to show this means 'in it'. The 'it' refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse, which is why we use the feminine form.
Changed the verb 'permit' into the noun 'permission', like changing 'allow' to 'allowance' in English
Added بِ at start to mean 'by' and ِ at end to show it connects to the next word (their Lord)

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does a lot of caring and nurturing
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/address to one's Lord

steps.m: their master who cares for them (referring to those who believed and did good deeds)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this refers to the believers mentioned earlier in the verse
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from 'to greet' into 'a greeting' by adding تَ at start and ة at end, with doubled ي in middle
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing something (in this case, being peace)
Added هُمْ to show the greeting belongs to them. This word is the subject, with سَلٰمٌ (peace) being what describes their greeting.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it shows where the believers will be (in the gardens).
The ها suffix refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah where the believers will dwell
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding alif after second letter turns verb 'to be peaceful' into noun 'peace'
Adding double dammah makes it 'a peace' instead of 'the peace'
This is the basic negation word that changes present tense verbs to past tense negative

steps.m: have not...? (expecting 'yes, it has' as answer)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the listener to agree. It's like saying 'Haven't you...?' in English when you want someone to agree that they have.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The hamza (أ) stays, and the ي changes to ى at the end.
Added ت at beginning to show 'you'. The hamza (أ) drops because it's between two vowels.
The ى at the end is dropped because this word comes before another word (كَيْفَ) in the ayah
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce this question word

steps.m: to hit, strike, set forth, or present (an example)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a complete past tense verb. In this ayah, Allah is setting forth or presenting an example (مَثَلًا), not physically striking.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks

This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it specific and definite. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject of ضَرَبَ (struck) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form

steps.m: an example (object of ضَرَبَ - set forth)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being set forth in Allah's comparison
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added -ah ending to make it mean 'a single word' instead of 'speaking'
Adds two small diagonal lines (tanween) at the end to show this word is receiving the action

steps.m: to be pure, pleasant, or good

steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to make the meaning stronger, showing this is something very pure or good. This word describes كَلِمَةً (word) in the ayah.
Added ة to match the feminine gender of كَلِمَةً (word) that this describes
Added ً (tanween fatha) to match the case ending of كَلِمَةً that this word describes
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added ة to make it refer to one specific tree

steps.m: like a tree (being compared to كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً - a good word)
steps.n: Added كَ ('like/similar to') at the start and tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show it's being compared to 'a good word' mentioned earlier

steps.m: to be pure, pleasant, or good

steps.n: The vowels show this describes a temporary quality

steps.m: very good, purely good (describing كَلِمَةً - word)
steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing this word is completely and truly good

steps.m: very good, purely good (feminine form matching كَلِمَةً - word)
steps.n: Added ة to match the feminine gender of كَلِمَةً (word) that it describes

steps.m: a very good, purely good (feminine form matching كَلِمَةً - word)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is describing an indefinite noun
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is doing something in the sentence - it's the subject of ثَابِتٌ (firmly fixed)
Added ها at the end to show this root belongs to the tree (شَجَرَة) mentioned earlier. This whole phrase أَصْلُهَا ثَابِتٌ means 'its root is firmly fixed'

steps.m: to be firmly fixed in place

steps.n: Added ا after ث and ِ after ب to show this describes something that has this quality. This word tells us what the tree's root (أصل) is like.
The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is describing something in a general way, like saying 'a firmly fixed thing' instead of 'the firmly fixed thing'

steps.m: to branch out, to rise up

steps.n: The root becomes a noun meaning a physical branch. This word refers to part of the good tree (شَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
The ها refers back to the good tree (شَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ) mentioned earlier. This word is in the nominative case (ُ) because it's the subject, like the word أَصْلُهَا (its root) before it.

steps.m: and its branch (in فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ - in the sky)
steps.n: The و connects this to أَصْلُهَا (its root) from the previous ayah, showing that while the tree's root is firm below, its branch extends into the sky
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the branches are 'in the sky'.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س because it comes after ال
Added ا after م to make it a strong noun meaning 'sky/heaven'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in/at)
Joining the separate root letters أ-ت-ي together forms the basic verb meaning 'to come'
The تُ prefix shows this is about a female subject (she) doing the action now
The sukun (ْ) shows there's no vowel sound between the first two root letters
The kasra makes this a Form IV verb, meaning 'to cause' something
The ي at the end shows this happens in the present time
The ُ marks make this a plural noun showing multiple types

steps.m: its fruit (object of تُؤْتِى - it gives)
steps.n: Added ها which shows this fruit belongs to something feminine (like a tree). This is what's being given in the phrase 'it gives its fruit'.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تُؤْتِىٓ (it gives)
Adding the kasra (ِ) vowel mark makes this a noun meaning 'time'

steps.m: a time/period (in كُلَّ حِينٍۭ - every time)
steps.n: Added tanwin to show this is indefinite. This word is part of the phrase كُلَّ حِينٍۭ meaning 'every time/period'.
Changed the verb into a noun that means the act of permission itself
Added بِ at the start to mean 'by' or 'with', and ِ at the end to show it's connected to the word رَبِّهَا (their Lord) that comes after it

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does complete nurturing and care

steps.m: her Lord (referring to the good word/tree mentioned before)
steps.n: The هَا means 'her/its' and refers back to the good word/tree. The ِ (kasra) under ّب shows this word follows إِذْنِ (permission) in the phrase 'by permission of her Lord'

steps.m: to present examples, to set forth (originally: to strike)
steps.n: The root has a literal meaning of striking, but in this context it means to present or set forth examples
Added يَ at the beginning to show who does the action (he), ِ in the middle and ُ at the end to show it's happening in the present. This word refers to Allah (ٱللَّهُ) who is setting forth examples.

steps.m: and He presents/sets forth (subject of ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with what came before it in the verse
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
The word for 'god' (إِلَه) became the special name 'Allah' by adding ال in a unique way (ٱ), doubling the ل, and adding ُ to show it's doing the action
Changed from basic root meaning 'compare' into a noun meaning 'example' by adding the vowels a-a
Changed to plural form by adding أ at start and ا after last letter
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's the object of the verb يَضْرِبُ (he sets forth)

steps.m: to be friendly or social with others

steps.n: Changes the concept of being social into a word for the social beings (humans/people)
Adds ال (the) at the start to make it 'the people' instead of just 'people'
Adds لِ meaning 'for/to' and ِ at the end because it follows لِ

steps.m: perhaps they/so that they (connected to يَتَذَكَّرُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the word is talking about (they/them). This connects directly to the verb يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (they remember/take heed) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense and changes it from doing the action to making others do it
Adding تَ at the beginning changes 'remind someone else' to 'remember/remind oneself' - like talking to yourself
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Adding ون shows that many people are doing the action

steps.m: to be like something or give an example of it

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a simple noun meaning 'example'

steps.m: and an example (that describes كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ - an evil word)
steps.n: The وَ means 'and' and connects this example to the previous one. This word introduces a comparison about evil words.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and change from verb to noun
Added ة to show this is one specific word
Added tanwin kasra to show this word describes the 'example' (مَثَلُ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective that describes how something is bad or harmful. This describes the word (كَلِمَةٍ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added ة to match with the feminine word كَلِمَةٍ (word) that it's describing
Added ٍ to show this adjective follows and describes كَلِمَةٍ (word)
Added vowels and ة at the end to make it mean one specific tree
Added tanwin kasra to show this tree is being described by something else in the sentence

steps.m: like a tree (being compared to كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ - an evil word)
steps.n: Added كَ to create a comparison with the evil word mentioned before
Forms an adjective that describes the word (كَلِمَةٍ) as being evil or corrupt
Adds ة to match the feminine gender of the word it's describing (كَلِمَةٍ)
Adds the genitive case ending to match كَلِمَةٍ which it describes
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Added ا and ت to make the word mean 'to pull out completely and forcefully'
Changed vowel to show passive voice - something being done to it rather than it doing something

steps.m: it (feminine) was forcefully uprooted (referring to كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ - evil word)
steps.n: Added ت at end to show feminine past tense, and changed initial alif to hamzat wasl. This word describes what happened to the evil word mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a preposition that shows the tree was uprooted starting from above the ground
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to create the noun meaning 'above'. This word follows مِن (from) in this ayah.

steps.m: above (after مِن and connected to الأرض)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows two things: 1) it comes after the word مِن (from), and 2) it connects to الأرض (the earth) to mean 'above the earth'
Added a short 'a' sound (fatha) to make it a complete word meaning earth/land
Added a silent mark (sukun) on the middle letter to complete the basic noun pattern
Adding definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: of the earth (possessed by فَوْقِ - above)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end shows this word is owned by or connected to the word فَوْقِ (above) that comes before it
This is a negative particle that makes something not true. In this ayah, it negates لَهَا (for it) to say the evil word has no stability/firmness.
Added fatha to the letter lam to connect it with the next part

steps.m: for it/it has (referring to the evil word كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the evil word (كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The fatha on ه helps connect it smoothly.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or moves away from. In this ayah, it connects with فَوْقِ (above) to show something was uprooted from above the earth.

steps.m: to settle or remain firmly in place

steps.n: Added the alif after second root letter to make it into a noun meaning 'place of settling'

steps.m: any firm foundation (of مَا لَهَا - what it has)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is any foundation in general, and it belongs to something else in the sentence (what the evil word has)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (ّ) on the middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: he makes very steady/firm (subject of يَفْعَلُ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra (ِ), and adding ُ at end. This word is one of the things Allah does, connected to يَفْعَلُ (he does) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is the basic form meaning 'who' that we start with
Added ين at the end to make it refer to many people instead of just one person
Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people we're talking about

steps.m: those who (referring to the believers mentioned in ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added final pronunciation marks and changed ال to ٱ for proper Quranic spelling. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) right after it, telling us which specific people are being talked about.
Added vowel marks (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it mean 'he trusted' in the past tense
Added alif after first letter to show causing or entering into a state

steps.m: they believed (those who اللَّهُ makes firm)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word describes the people that Allah makes firm in their faith.
Changed from an action (saying something) to the thing itself (speech/statement)
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific speech or statement
Added بِ at the start to show this speech is being used as a tool or method

steps.m: by the firm speech (connected to الثَّابِتِ)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this word is connected to الثَّابِتِ (firm/established) that comes after it, creating the meaning 'the firm speech'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the word pattern to show something that has this quality

steps.m: the firm one, the stable one
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific/definite

steps.m: of the firm, stable one (describes القول/the word)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this describes 'the word' (القول) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it shows location within 'the life'
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it as 'fi'
Changed the ي to ى (alif maqsurah) because it comes at the end of the word
Changed from action (living) to thing (life) by adding و and ة at the end
Added ال to specify this particular life, referring to worldly life mentioned in the ayah
Added kasra to show this word is connected to الدُّنْيَا (worldly) that comes after it in the ayah
Changed the root letters to make a feminine word meaning 'closer/lower' - often used to describe worldly life compared to the afterlife

steps.m: the worldly life (as opposed to the afterlife)
steps.n: Added 'the' to show we're talking about this specific worldly life. The shadda (ّ) appears because when you add ال, the ل combines with the د
Basic preposition meaning 'in' - used to show location or being inside something
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Root letters got an alif and vowels added to make a word meaning 'last' or 'final' in a sequence
Adding ة makes this refer specifically to the final life or realm that comes after this world
Adding ال makes this specifically refer to the Islamic concept of the afterlife. The ِ at the end shows it follows the word في (in) in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
The damma shows this is in the basic present tense form
Adds 'and' (و) at the start to connect this action with what came before
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject

steps.m: to do wrong, to be unjust

steps.n: Changed the word to show the person who does the action
Added ين to show there are many wrongdoers

steps.m: the wrongdoers (who are misled by يُضِلُّ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and shadda on ظ. This word is the object of يُضِلُّ (leads astray) in this ayah.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and adding ُ at end. This word describes what Allah does, as mentioned in اللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ (Allah what He wills)
Added و to connect this to the previous statement about Allah. This continues describing what Allah does.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of يُثَبِّتُ - makes firm)
steps.n: Changed to the unique name of the One God. The ُ at the end shows this is the doer of the action 'makes firm' in this ayah.
Basic form of the relative pronoun without any vowel marks
Added fatha to show this is the object (thing being acted upon) in the phrase 'whatever He wills'

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel marks
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action
Added long ا to show the action takes time or is ongoing
This is the basic negation particle that changes a present tense verb to past tense negative

steps.m: is it not true that... (expecting 'yes, it is true')
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects a positive answer. In this ayah, it's asking 'Don't you see?' expecting the answer 'Yes, I do see.'

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added ت at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action in the present
The ى is dropped because it comes after أَلَمْ which requires a shortened form of the verb
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects the verb تَرَ (you see) to ٱلَّذِينَ (those who), showing who is being seen.
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun form
Added ال (the) at the beginning. The ل combines with ذ to make a stronger ذّ sound.
Added ينَ at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb بَدَّلُوا (they exchanged) in this ayah.

steps.m: to exchange or replace one thing with another

steps.n: The doubled middle letter د shows this was done thoroughly and deliberately. This word connects to نِعْمَتَ (blessing) which comes after it in the ayah.
The suffix وا shows a group of people (they) did this action. They are exchanging Allah's نِعْمَتَ (blessing) mentioned right after this word.

steps.m: to give comfort, blessing, or favor

steps.n: Added kasra and sukun to form a noun meaning 'blessing'. The ة shows it's one specific blessing.
The word gets a fatha at the end because it's the object (the thing being exchanged) in the sentence
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'god' in general

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it specific and the shadda emphasizes its uniqueness. The kasra at the end shows it's being acted upon by something before it in the sentence.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that names the action itself, like changing 'reject' to 'rejection'
Added ًا to show this word is receiving the action of بَدَّلُوا (they exchanged). In this ayah, it shows what the people exchanged Allah's favor for.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning and shadda on middle letter to show causing someone else to do the action
Added و at beginning to connect this action with previous actions in the verse

steps.m: and they made (their people) settle (referring to قَوْمَهُمْ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. The word قَوْمَهُمْ (their people) shows who was made to settle.

steps.m: people/nation (object of أَحَلُّوا - they caused to settle)
steps.n: The root meaning of standing together becomes a noun for a group that stands as one. This word is receiving the action of أَحَلُّوا (they caused to settle) in this ayah.
Adds -a ending to show this word is receiving the action
Adds possessive pronoun suffix meaning 'their'

steps.m: to move in a circle, rotate, or turn around

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Changed the root into a noun meaning the result of being ruined

steps.m: the ruin (object of دَارَ meaning 'home/abode of')
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to the word دَارَ (home/abode)
This is the basic form of the word, which comes from Hebrew 'Gehinnom'. It doesn't have an Arabic root.

steps.m: Hell (the object being entered in يَصْلَوْنَهَا - they will enter it)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. The word connects to يَصْلَوْنَهَا (they will enter it) which comes right after.
This root relates to exposure to heat or fire
Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening now or will happen
Changed to plural form by adding وْنَ to show many people doing the action
Added هَا which refers back to جَهَنَّمَ (Hell) mentioned earlier in the verse
Changed the root into a special word that shows strong disapproval or criticism. This form is used to say something is really bad.

steps.m: and how terrible! (criticizing جَهَنَّمَ as a place to end up)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this criticism to what came before it, emphasizing how bad Hell is as a destination
This root is about something becoming fixed or settled in one place

steps.m: place of settling/staying (subject of بِئْسَ - 'how evil is')
steps.n: The alif after the second root letter changes this from an action (settling) to a place where that action happens (settling place). This word is being described as evil in the phrase بِئْسَ ٱلْقَرَارُ (how evil is the settling place).

steps.m: the place of settling/staying (subject of بِئْسَ - 'how evil is')
steps.n: Adding the definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: the place of settling/staying (subject of بِئْسَ - 'how evil is')
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this word is the subject being described as evil in the phrase بِئْسَ ٱلْقَرَارُ (how evil is the settling place).

steps.m: to make or set (something as something else)

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا to show a group of people did this action (referring to the disbelievers in this ayah)

steps.m: and they made/set (أَندَادًا rivals for/to اللَّهِ Allah)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this with previous statement. The word is followed by أَندَادًا (rivals) which they set up for/to اللَّهِ (Allah).
Added لِ at the beginning to show the meaning 'for' or 'to'. The ا from الله is removed when لِ is added.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلُوا (they made/set up).

steps.m: to rival or be equal to

steps.n: The doubled middle letter (ّ) and fatha (َ) vowel transform the root into a noun meaning 'equal/rival'
Changing the vowel to kasra (ِ) makes this a noun meaning something similar or equal
The word نِدّ (equal/rival) becomes plural by adding أَ at the start and اد at the end to make أَندَاد (rivals/equals)
Added ًا to show this word is the object of جَعَلُوا (they made/set up) - meaning they set up these rivals for Allah

steps.m: to stray from the right path

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters and doubled the last ل to make 'he strayed' in past tense
Adding hamza at start makes the verb causative - showing someone making another person do the action
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: so that they cause (others) to stray
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose/intent

steps.m: so that they cause (others) to stray (from سَبِيلِهِۦ)
steps.n: The ل got stronger (shadda) and a small و was added at the end - both are just writing style changes that don't change the meaning
This is a preposition that shows movement or turning away from something. In this ayah, it shows people turning away from Allah's path (سَبِيلِهِ).

steps.m: to make a way or path

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it. This word comes after عَن (away from) in the ayah.
Added هِۦ to show the path belongs to Allah. This whole phrase عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ means 'away from His path'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by dropping first vowel and changing middle vowel to 'oo' sound
The middle weak letter 'و' is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say

steps.m: to enjoy, take pleasure in, or benefit from

steps.n: Added تَ at beginning and doubled middle letter ت to show doing something a lot to yourself. Like telling someone 'really enjoy yourself!'
Added plural masculine command suffix وا to make it a command to multiple people
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'surely' in English.

Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about people taking partners with Allah and leading others astray, showing the consequence of these actions.
Added مَ at start to show place where action happens, and changed middle vowel to ِ
Added كُم to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed. This word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah.
The sukun (circle mark) shows where to pause when reading
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱلنَّارِ (the Fire), showing where people are heading.
The root letters were shaped into a noun by adding the vowel marks. The middle letter و became ا to make it easier to say.
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda
Added kasra at the end because this word shows where something is going - it's the destination being referred to by مَصِيرَكُمْ (your destination)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to عِبَادِىَ)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by adding damma on first letter and dropping the weak middle letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to His servants.
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to make the singular noun form
Changes vowel pattern and adds long alif to make plural form
Adds emphatic لِّ at beginning to mean 'surely to/for'
Adds ى at the end to show possession (my/mine)
Adds fatha at end to show this is addressing the servants directly
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make it relative
Adding ال makes it specifically refer to someone or something

steps.m: those who (describing the believers in ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one. This word describes the believers mentioned right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: those who (describing the believers in ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding the proper pronunciation marks and changing ال to ٱ for proper Quranic spelling
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif makes it mean 'to believe/have faith' instead of just 'to feel safe' - this is a common pattern to make new meanings in Arabic
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to يُقِيمُوا (they establish) in the ayah, showing these are the same people doing both actions.

steps.m: to establish or make something stand firm

steps.n: Added يُ at the start to show this is happening now/future, and the vowel pattern changes the middle weak letter و to ي
The وا shows this is about multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to connect with something through bending/bowing

steps.n: Basic root letters got vowels to make a past tense verb meaning 'he prayed'
Changed from 'to pray' into 'a prayer' by adding ة at the end and making the last vowel longer
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the ص with shadda because it comes after ٱل
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb يُقِيمُوا۟ (they establish)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added و with fatha to connect this action with what came before
Changed from 'he spends' to 'they spend' by adding وا at the end - makes it plural
This word combines two basic particles: مِن meaning 'from' and ما meaning 'what'. In this ayah, it refers to spending from what Allah has provided.
When مِن comes before a word starting with م, the ن merges into the م, shown by the ّ (shadda) mark. This is just a pronunciation change, not a meaning change.

steps.m: to provide with what is needed

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed last vowel to sukun to prepare for adding suffixes
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُمْ to show who received the provision
Connected the letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
The verb 'he hid' becomes the noun 'secret' by doubling the middle letter (with shaddah) and changing the first vowel to kasra

steps.m: secretly (describes how something is done in يُنفِقُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif changes this into a word that describes how the action of spending (يُنفِقُوا۟) is done - in secret
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added a long 'aa' sound in the middle and an 'i' sound at the end to change from 'he did it' to 'doing it'
Added ية to create abstract noun
Added و to connect with previous word سِرًّا (secretly)
Added tanween fatha to show this describes how the spending mentioned in يُنفِقُوا۟ is done
This is a particle meaning 'from' or 'of'

The م gets doubled (receives shadda) because it comes after a word ending in a vowel. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning. This word connects to قَبْلِ (before) which comes after it.

steps.m: to come before or face towards

steps.n: Added vowel marks to the root letters to make a word meaning 'before' in time or place
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to 'day' (يَوْمٌ) that comes after it in the ayah
This is a particle that introduces what comes next. Here it connects to the verb يَأْتِىَ (comes) to show 'that [a day] comes'
Connected the root letters together
Added ي at the start to show 'he' doing the action in present time, with vowels to make it a complete word
Added fatha at end to show this is in the subjunctive mood

steps.m: day (the period of time that passes)
steps.n: Root letters put together with vowels to make the word for 'day'

steps.m: a day (that will come before لَّا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ - there will be no trading in it)
steps.n: Added the double-damma mark to show this is talking about any day, not a specific one. This day is being described in the next part of the verse as having no trading in it.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it in the ayah
The 'l' sound is made stronger (doubled) because it comes after a vowel sound - this is just for easier pronunciation
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The fatha on ب and sukun on ي show this is a noun meaning the act of selling.
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of the negation لا (there is no). This word appears in the phrase لا بَيْعٌ (there is no selling/trading).
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: in it (referring to يَوْمٌ - that day)
steps.n: Added the attached pronoun ه (hi) meaning 'it' to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to يَوْمٌ (that day) mentioned earlier.
This is a negative particle that negates the word بَيْعٌ (trade/selling) in this ayah
Adding the conjunction و connects this negative particle with what came before

steps.m: to be close friends with someone

steps.n: Changes from verb to plural noun meaning spaces or gaps
Added alif to make it mean close friendship instead of just gaps/spaces
Added tanween dammah to make the word indefinite
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and merged the letters together in a special way because this is Allah's name
Added the shadda to emphasize the ل and added ُ to show this is the doer of the action 'created' (خَلَقَ) in this ayah
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects a description to someone or something

steps.m: the one who (subject of خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it specific. This word points to Allah who is mentioned before it, and connects to the verb خَلَقَ (created) after it, telling us what Allah did.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word tells us what Allah (ٱللَّهُ) did.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun meaning sky/heaven
Added وات (waat) at the end to make it mean 'many skies' instead of just one sky
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word belongs to/is owned by something
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific earth/ground rather than any earth/ground

steps.m: the earth (object of خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: Adding fatHa shows this is what is being created in the phrase 'created the heavens and the earth'

steps.m: and the earth (object of خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: وَ means 'and', ٱلْ means 'the', and َ shows it's the object of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb

Adding يَ at the start changes it from 'he reminded' to 'he remembers/gets reminded' - like changing from doing the action to receiving it

steps.m: so that he remembers/is reminded intensively
steps.n: Adding لِ shows this is happening for a purpose

steps.m: and so that he remembers/is reminded intensively
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous phrase in the verse
This is a special plural noun that means 'people who possess something'. In this ayah, it's connected to الْأَلْبٰبِ meaning 'those who possess understanding/intellect'.
Added small circles above some letters to help with pronunciation. These marks don't change the meaning.
Forms the basic noun meaning 'mind' or 'intellect' by adding damma and doubling the middle letter
Plural form made by adding أَ at the start and changing the middle to اب
Adds kasra at the end to show this word follows 'of' or belongs to something

steps.m: of the minds/intellects (referring to people of understanding)
steps.n: Adds ال to make it specific, referring to people who use their minds to understand
